title.rendered: Cinnamon Creek Sale Provides Critical Support

content.rendered:
Utah’s Reservoirs and Public Schools Benefit Big

SALT LAKE CITY (Nov. 17, 2021) – The State of Utah School and Institutional Trust Lands Administration (SITLA) sold its largest parcel to date for $20,000,013 yesterday. The winning bid for the 8,100-acre Cinnamon Creek parcel was relayed to the agency by EnergyNet, the online auction and land sales company that managed the sealed bid sale.

“This is a big win for our beneficiaries,” stated Michelle McConkie, an Assistant Director at SITLA who oversees the agency’s Surface Management Group. “It’s about a 50/50 split with reservoirs and public schools. This piece of land brought in about $20,000 last year; with this sale, the beneficiaries are looking at millions in revenue, an amount that would take decades to generate in its current state.”

Utah trust lands, unlike public lands, were specifically allocated by Congress at statehood to generate revenue to support designated state institutions. The beneficiaries of trust land revenue include public schools, hospitals, juvenile justice services, teaching colleges, universities, and reservoirs. Utah’s constitution and statutes mandate that SITLA manage the lands and revenues generated from them in the most profitable manner possible by planning for the current beneficiaries while preserving trust assets for future beneficiaries.

“Utah experienced some of the worst water supply conditions this year than most of us have seen in our lifetimes,” said Candice Hasenyager, director of the Utah Division of Water Resources. “Many of our reservoirs have hit record-low water levels. Looking ahead, we need to reduce how much water we use and increase our storage. SITLA has always looked out for us, and this sale couldn’t come at a better time.”

Paula Plant, Director of the School Children’s Trust at the Utah State Board of Education, said, “We’re grateful to SITLA for making this sale such a success! This sale will generate additional funds for Utah school students, not just next year but every year going forward. In Utah, every public school has a council that directs how their portion of the trust funds are spent. Councils may hire teachers, reading specialists, purchase technology, provide hands-on STEAM projects and equipment for children with special needs. The funds are spent for the unique needs of each school.”

title.rendered: Auction Nets $1.37M for K12, School for the Deaf

content.rendered: SITLA sold seven trust land parcels at its biannual public auction this week. The land auction generated $1,372,750 for two trust land endowments, K12 public schools and Utah School for the Deaf. All but one of the eight parcels offered by SITLA sold during the agency’s first online public auction. The 1.47-acre Highway 91 parcel in Iron County did not sell. The trust land parcels sold totaled approximately 1,040 surface acres in five counties throughout the state. By law, SITLA retains all mineral estate. For a full auction summary, visit the Land Auction page. Proceeds from the sale are deposited directly into the $2.5 billion permanent endowments for K12 public schools and the Utah School for the Deaf. These institutions receive annual interest earnings from their endowments to use as they see fit. Utah’s K-12 public school system received $88.83 million this school year, which was used largely for teacher and aide salaries, and classroom technology. On behalf of trust land beneficiaries, SITLA generated $75.25 million from energy, mineral, surface, and real estate development, and land sales last fiscal year.

title.rendered: Competitive Mineral Lease Offering Earns $400K for Trust Beneficiaries

content.rendered:
The SITLA Oil and Gas, and Minerals groups held their quarterly lease offering in October generating approximately $400,000 for three trust land beneficiaries: public schools, state reservoirs, and the University of Utah.

The October lease offering generated $397,579.12 in bonus bids, largely for oil and gas, on approximately 6,000 acres of trust lands within Duchesne, Uintah, and San Juan counties. The offering also resulted in two metalliferous minerals leases in Duchesne and Utah counties.

Proceeds from the quarterly offerings are deposited into individual permanent funds for trust land beneficiaries, which total a combined $2.5 billion. Beneficiaries received $95 million in annual earnings from their permanent endowment funds last fiscal year.

The next lease offering is tentatively scheduled for January 2021. SITLA online auctions are conducted at EnergyNet.com. For more information visit the SITLA Competitive Mineral Lease Offering site.

content.rendered: SITLA offers eight properties at its online land sale auction November 12-18, 2020. Visit the auction site for information on the upcoming sale, including parcel maps, photographs, descriptions, and bid start/end times.

title.rendered: SITLA Trust Land Auction Earns $344K for Schools, UofU College of Mines

content.rendered: SITLA held its biannual public auction this week generating $343,900 for two beneficiary endowments, public schools and the University of Utah College of Mines and Earth Sciences. The seven parcels sold totaled a combined 315 acres and are located in Duchesne, Emery, Garfield, and Sevier counties. By law, SITLA retains the mineral estate. All proceeds from the sale are deposited into the permanent endowments for each beneficiary. These institutions receive annual interest earnings from their endowments to use as they see fit. Utah’s K-12 public school system received $82.7 million this school year, which was spent largely for teacher and aide salaries, and classroom technology. SITLA manages Utah’s 3.4 million acres of trust lands on behalf of 12 beneficiaries designated at statehood. This fiscal year, SITLA generated $89.98 million from energy, mineral, surface, and real estate development on trust lands.

title.rendered: SITLA Auction Nets $4 Million for Public School, Other Endowments

content.rendered: SITLA sold 17 trust land parcels at its biannual public auction last week generating $4,013,060 for the endowments for public schools, state hospitals, teaching colleges, and reservoirs. The 17 parcels sold at public auction totaled just under 3,000 surface acres in nine counties throughout the state. By law, SITLA retains the mineral estate. The 240-acre Virgin Corridor parcel in Washington County, with a minimum acceptable price of $1.26 million, did not sell. All proceeds from the sale are deposited into the $2.5 billion permanent endowments for public schools, Miners Hospital at the University of Utah Medical Center, teaching colleges at state universities, and reservoirs. These institutions receive annual interest income from their endowments to use as they see fit. Utah’s K-12 public school system received $74.8 million this school year, which was used largely for teacher and aide salaries, and classroom technology. On behalf of trust land beneficiaries, SITLA generated $73 million from energy, mineral, surface, and real estate development, and land sales last fiscal year.

title.rendered: October 24 Trust Lands Auction to Benefit Public Schools, State Hospitals

content.rendered: The School and Institutional Trust Lands Administration (SITLA) will sell 18 trust land parcels at its biannual public auction at 10 a.m. Wednesday, October 24 in the Deer Valley Room at the Sheraton Salt Lake City Hotel located at 150 West 500 South. This is the largest sale of trust lands in recent history with parcels available in 11 counties. All proceeds from the sale will be deposited into the permanent endowments for public schools, Miners Hospital at the University of Utah Medical Center, teaching colleges at state universities, and reservoirs. For more information on the following parcels, please visit the land sale website or call 801-538-5163 and toll-free 877-526-3725. Green River East C-26797: Emery County 40 acres | Beneficiary: Reservoirs Indian Creek C-26794: Beaver County 199.29 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools Jenson Canyon Parcels: Duchesne County C-26781-A 480 acres Beneficiary: Miners Hospital and Public Schools C-26781-B 320 acres Beneficiary: Public Schools La Verkin Parcel C-26774: Washington County 40 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools Milburn Parcel C-26790 Sanpete County 160 acres | Beneficiary: Teaching Colleges Minnie Maud C-26793 Carbon County 40 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools Rockville Corridor C-26770 Washington County 298.66 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools Shurtz Canyon C-26787 Iron County 80 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools Square Mountain C26788 Iron County 616.55 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools Virgin Corridor C-26769 Washington County 240 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools Wales Parcel C-26789 Sanpete County 10 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools White River C-26782 Wasatch County 153.91 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools Widtsoe Townsites Garfield County C-26786-A .56 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools C-26786-B 1.0 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools C-26786-C 1.0 acre | Beneficiary: Public Schools Yellow Creek Parcels Garfield County C-26785-A 240 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools C-26785-B 279.15 acres | Beneficiary: Public Schools
